content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

June 26, 1991

Julie Messenger  
City of Riverside  
3900 Main Street  
Riverside, CA 92522

Re: Combined Campaign Report  
Our File No. I-91-300

Dear Ms. Messenger:

This is to confirm advice provided to you on June 4, 1991, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1 This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205.

Section 84205 states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title.

Committees formed or existing primarily to support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure must file a campaign statement 21 days after any petitions are filed, or 21 days after the deadline for filing petitions, whichever is earlier. (Section 84200.5(f).) Effective January 1, 1991, committees primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of a ballot measure are required to file semiannual campaign statements during the qualification period in addition to the 21-day post-qualification statement. (Section 84200.)

You indicated that a committee formed to support the qualification of a local ballot measure filed petitions to qualify the measure on June 10, 1991. The 21-day post-qualification statement will cover the period January 1, 1991 through June 24, 1991, and will be due on July 1, 1991.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

However, because the 21-day statement and the semiannual statement due for the first six months of 1991 (Section 84200) cover almost identical periods, it would be appropriate for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of the ballot measure to combine those two reports.

Therefore, we advised you that the combined statement should cover the period January 1, 1991 through June 30, 1991, and should be filed no later than July 8, 1991.

If you have any questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Sandra L. Taylor
Political Reform Consultant
